quizzes.lessonTitle: Australian-specific hazards — bushfire ash and asbestos-in-debris
quizzes.title: Fire & Smoke Damage Restoration Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Which smoke residue type results from a fast, high-heat fire of natural materials and is generally the easiest to remove?
options: Wet smoke residue
Protein smoke residue
Dry smoke residue
Fuel-oil smoke residue

questions.questionText: A room shows a near-invisible, yellowed film with an extremely strong odour out of proportion to visible residue, following a cooking incident. This is most likely:
options: Dry smoke residue
Protein smoke residue
Fuel-oil smoke residue
Wet smoke residue

questions.questionText: Why is identifying the smoke residue type the most important assessment decision before cleaning begins?
options: It determines the insurance excess payable
It has no real effect on the job — all residue is cleaned the same way
It determines the cleaning chemistry and method used, and the wrong choice can smear or drive residue deeper into the surface
It only matters for contents, never for structural surfaces

questions.questionText: Why does smoke commonly contaminate rooms with no visible fire damage?
options: It does not — only the room of origin is ever affected
Heat, buoyancy, the stack effect, pressure differentials and the building’s own HVAC system carry smoke well beyond the point of origin
Smoke only spreads if every internal door is left open
Smoke migration only occurs in single-storey buildings

questions.questionText: A fire-affected building’s HVAC system was running during the fire. What is the correct action before the system is used again?
options: Run it as normal — HVAC systems are not affected by smoke
Increase the fan speed to clear the smoke faster
Have it professionally cleaned and filters replaced before restarting, to avoid re-contaminating already-cleaned rooms
Disconnect it permanently regardless of the extent of contamination

questions.questionText: Why is corrosion control on exposed metal treated as time-critical, first-24–48-hour emergency mitigation?
options: Acidic soot residue can actively corrode appliances, wiring and electronics within hours to days if left untreated
Metal surfaces are the only thing insurers assess
Corrosion only matters if the fire was caused by an electrical fault
It is not time-critical — it can be scheduled with the general cleaning scope

questions.questionText: In structural deodorisation, why must source removal and cleaning happen before deodorisation techniques are applied?
options: Deodorisation is always done first to make the space pleasant to clean
Masking an odour source that has not been physically removed only hides the problem temporarily
Deodorisation equipment cannot operate in a dirty structure
There is no required sequence — either can be done first

questions.questionText: Firefighting water has left a structure wet, and mould is later found during the dry-out. What is the correct approach to airflow?
options: Run air movers directly across the mouldy surfaces to dry them faster
Use air filtration devices (AFDs) and dehumidifiers only; do not run air movers until the area is cleaned and cleared
Increase airflow and skip dehumidification since the fire has already dried most surfaces
Ignore the mould, since it is a fire job rather than a water damage job

questions.questionText: Ash from a structure fire is found to include burnt CCA-treated timber (fencing or decking). What does this mean for handling the ash?
options: It can be swept up dry like ordinary wood ash
It should be treated the same as any US wildfire ash guidance describes
It may carry arsenic, copper and chromium compounds and must be handled as contaminated waste, not dry-swept, and disposed of per state EPA requirements
It is only a concern if the timber was still burning when crews arrived

questions.questionText: During debris removal on a fire job in an older Australian building, a technician suspects a broken sheet of fibro (asbestos-containing material) among the debris. What is the correct response?
options: Bag it with the rest of the fire debris and continue the job
Wet it down and sweep it up quickly to limit dust
Stop work immediately, do not disturb the material, and hand the area over to the separate licensed asbestos removal process
Continue cleaning around it as long as PPE is worn
